checks.name: attempt-scoped human authorization
checks.summary: An independent human hash-pins the complete request only after the exact Run and Attempt exist; retries require distinct attempt-scoped authorization.

checks.name: atomic lease and fencing authority
checks.summary: Worktree, file, and artifact resources are acquired atomically in deterministic order; every mutation revalidates exact scheduler and delivery fences.

checks.name: worker and filesystem boundary
checks.summary: Workers receive only pre-created approved roots and cannot commit, validate, publish, or convert symlink, hidden-index, ignored, or out-of-scope mutations into success.

checks.name: publication and recovery integrity
checks.summary: Publication journals immutable authority before CAS, rechecks leases and evidence inside the fence, publishes only a namespaced ref, and recovers from canonical receipts plus exact Git observations.

checks.name: failure preservation
checks.summary: Dirty, unverifiable, conflicted, or reconciliation-marked attempts retain bytes and authority until fail-closed quarantine or explicit administrative reconciliation completes.

residual_risks: Automatic physical deletion of worktrees remains disabled until process quiescence and live-lease absence can be proven; clean and contaminated bytes are conservatively quarantined or retained.
Worker execution is an integrity boundary, not a production confidentiality sandbox; dedicated disposable hosts and deployment-managed egress controls remain required until Sprint 12.
A durable publication journal can reconcile after external evidence files disappear, but long-term evidence retention depends on the object store planned for Sprint 07.
